_comment: Set 068 S2 SYMMETRIC re-grade overrides for the PATH-AWARE (B1/B2) x CROSS-FILE cells. The Set 067 audit (../../067-pull-verifier-adapter-experiment-a/experiment-a/audit.json) applied the strict 'names the actual defect mechanism, not a token/substring artifact' rule ONLY to routed (A1/A2) x cross-file cells. The 0.21.1 erratum recorded that asymmetry. This file holds the SAME rule applied to the path-aware cross-file cells. The re-grade (regrade.py) applies BOTH audit files. Each override quotes the deciding text so the cross-provider verifier can check it against ../../067-.../experiment-a/raw/. Rule (identical to the routed audit): a catch of a CROSS-FILE defect counts only if the arm's text identifies the ACTUAL seeded mechanism, not a token match carried by a DIFFERENT finding.

kept_notes.B1:D5: KEEP. B1 k1/k2/k3 name the exact mechanism: two operators named 'minus' in operators.py and REGISTRY={op.name:op} overwriting/dropping binary subtraction. Mechanism-grounded (read omitted operators.py).
kept_notes.B2:D5: KEEP. B2 k1/k2/k3 name the duplicate 'minus' name collision and the REGISTRY overwrite making binary subtraction inaccessible.
kept_notes.B1:D6: KEEP. B1 k1/k2/k3 name the seeded mechanism: PRECEDENCE keyed by NAMES ('add','minus',...) while precedence(symbol) is called with SYMBOLS ('+','*'), so precedence('+') returns 0. Same mechanism the routed audit KEPT for A1:D6.
kept_notes.B2:D6: KEEP (via k3). B2 k3 Low finding names it: 'precedence(symbol) states it takes an operator symbol. However ... keyed by operator names, not symbols (e.g., add, not +)'. (B2 k1 instead describes a D5-consequence on precedence('minus'); union-over-K credits the arm because k3 names the real D6 mechanism, symmetric with crediting routed at the union level.)
kept_notes.B1:D9: KEEP. B1 k1/k2/k3 name analyzer.all_refs returning only collect_assignment_refs and silently dropping call/return refs (the C9 mechanism; needs omitted analyzer.py).
kept_notes.B2:D9: KEEP. B2 k1/k2/k3 name all_refs returning only collect_assignment_refs, omitting call/return refs.
kept_notes.B1:D10: KEEP. B1 k1/k2/k3 name build_index doing name=str(ref) on a {name,line} dict, indexing the stringified dict instead of ref['name']. Same mechanism the routed audit KEPT for A1:D10.
kept_notes.B2:D10: KEEP. B2 k1/k2/k3 name name=str(ref) stringifying the reference dict instead of ref['name'].
kept_notes.B1:D11: KEEP. B1 k1/k2/k3 name count_statements returning len(source.splitlines()), counting blank lines against the documented exclusion.
kept_notes.B2:D11: KEEP. B2 k1/k2/k3 name the same len(source.splitlines()) blank-line miscount.
kept_notes.B2:D12: KEEP. B2 k2 names the exact mechanism: collect_call_refs only parses a single bare-identifier argument via isidentifier(), dropping no-arg/multi-arg/expression calls.
kept_notes.B1:D13: KEEP. B1 k1/k2/k3 name to_record using data.get('version',1), injecting a default for a schema-REQUIRED-no-default field (needs omitted schema.py).
kept_notes.B2:D13: KEEP. B2 k1/k2/k3 name data.get('version',1) defaulting a required field with no default.

reject_notes.B1:D12: REJECT (token artifact carried by the D9 finding). B1's tree3 findings across k1/k2/k3 are: count_statements (D11), all_refs-drops-call/return-refs (D9), build_index str(ref) (D10), and dedup. The D12 predicate ([collect_call_refs|call ref|...] AND [nested|multi|...|single identifier|isidentifier|dropped|silently]) is satisfied ONLY because B1's D9 finding says 'Call references from collect_call_refs ... are silently dropped' -- i.e. all_refs drops the WHOLE call-ref category (D9), NOT collect_call_refs's own single-bare-identifier / isidentifier() narrowing (the seeded D12). B1 never names the isidentifier()/multi-argument limitation in any replicate. This is exactly the substring/cross-finding-token failure mode the routed audit corrected (cf. A2:D10 'contraDICTs', A1:D13 token spillover). Removing it. (Contrast B2:D12 KEPT: B2 k2 explicitly names the isidentifier() single-argument limitation.)
